created_from: User prompt: what is the minimal conformal non-abelian model that can explain the NANOGrav signal?
warnings: The exact target model has already been studied for the NANOGrav 12.5-year FOPT interpretation in arXiv:2109.11558, so model-level novelty is low.
INSPIRE-HEP pages were JavaScript-only in this environment; arXiv metadata and full-text PDF/HTML were used for citation verification and extraction.
The exact-model paper contains a convention tension between its stated physical vector mass M_ZD = gD M / 2 and its displayed field-dependent mass m_D^2(phi) = g_D^2 phi^2 / 2.
Benchmark points from Abelian conformal models are not directly portable to the SU(2)_D doublet model.
approximations: No new FOPT or PTA numerical scan was performed; benchmark hints are extracted from literature only.
For papers where only metadata/abstract was needed, no benchmark formulas were inferred beyond accessible full text.

queries: conformal SU(2) dark sector first order phase transition NANOGrav
classically conformal non-Abelian dark sector NANOGrav gravitational waves
SU(2) dark gauge complex scalar doublet Coleman Weinberg gravitational waves first order phase transition
dark conformal phase transition NANOGrav PTA MeV
INSPIRE classically conformal dark SU(2) NANOGrav phase transition
searched_sources.notes: INSPIRE web content required JavaScript; used as search confirmation only, not for formulas or benchmarks.

papers.title: A first order dark SU(2)_D phase transition with vector dark matter in the light of NANOGrav 12.5 yr data
papers.brief_description: Exact core target model: classically conformal dark SU(2)_D with one complex scalar doublet, negligible Higgs portal, and a sub-electroweak first-order phase transition compared with NANOGrav 12.5-year preferred regions.
papers.model_studied: Dark SU(2)_D gauge extension of the SM with one complex scalar doublet Phi, all SM fields neutral under SU(2)_D, classically conformal tree-level scalar potential, and negligible Higgs portal coupling for the FOPT analysis.
papers.similarity_to_target: Same gauge group, same minimal scalar representation, same classical conformal setup, same approximate portal decoupling, same PTA/NANOGrav FOPT target. Differences may lie in convention choices, RGE/effective-potential implementation, and inclusion of vector dark matter relic production.
experimental_constraints: Planck dark matter relic abundance context
Future PTA sensitivity including SKA/IPTA in the paper discussion

benchmark_points.label: Figure 1 benchmark
benchmark_points.notes: Directly quoted from the paper's Figure 1 caption. Convert M_ZD to vD only after reconciling vector-mass normalization conventions.

benchmark_points.label: NANOGrav scan range

parameters.T_star: sub-electroweak to MeV/sub-GeV region in the plotted NANOGrav comparison
parameters.M_ZD: shown as color scale in the paper's alpha*-T* plots

benchmark_points.notes: Figure 2 scans gD in the range corresponding to alphaD in [0.01, 0.2] and overlays NANOGrav confidence regions.

assumptions: Landau gauge for the effective-potential calculation.
Negligible Higgs portal coupling lambda2 for the phase-transition analysis.
Classical conformal invariance removes bare scalar mass terms.
Daisy corrections included in the finite-temperature potential.
The paper compares to NANOGrav 12.5-year FOPT interpretations, not a 15-year PTArcade likelihood.

papers.title: Sub-GeV dark matter and nano-Hertz gravitational waves from a classically conformal dark sector
papers.brief_description: Classically conformal hidden U(1)' dark sector giving a MeV-scale FOPT and nano-Hertz PTA gravitational waves, combined with dark matter and laboratory/cosmological constraints.
papers.model_studied: Hidden U(1)' classically conformal dark sector with dark photon mediator, stable fermion dark matter, and kinetic mixing portal to the Standard Model.
papers.similarity_to_target: Shares classical conformal symmetry breaking, PTA-scale FOPT, MeV-scale transition logic, and dark-sector phenomenology, but the gauge group and matter content differ from the target SU(2)_D doublet model.
experimental_constraints: Dark photon searches including beam dumps and BaBar/NA64-type constraints as discussed in the paper
Cosmological constraints on late dark-sector decays

benchmark_points.label: PTA-scale qualitative target
benchmark_points.notes: Adaptable scale guidance only; not a direct SU(2)_D benchmark.

assumptions: Kinetic mixing portal does not violate the dark-sector conformal setup in that model.
Abelian vector masses/couplings cannot be directly mapped to the non-Abelian target without a separate calculation.

papers.title: NANOGrav Signal from a Dark Conformal Phase Transition
papers.brief_description: Nearly conformal confining dark sector/dilaton phase transition proposed as an explanation of the NANOGrav signal.
papers.model_studied: A nearly conformal dark sector described by a shallow dilaton/radion potential generated by dark Yang-Mills dynamics, with dark-sector temperature possibly different from the visible-sector temperature.
papers.similarity_to_target: Same broad conformal dark-sector PTA target, but not a perturbative Higgsed SU(2)_D scalar-doublet gauge theory.
assumptions: Confining/dilaton dynamics are not directly mapped to the target perturbative scalar-doublet potential.

papers.title: Supercooled phase transitions in conformal dark sectors explain NANOGrav data
papers.brief_description: Conformal dark U(1)' models with supercooled first-order phase transitions fitted to NANOGrav data.
papers.model_studied: Conformal Abelian U(1)' dark-sector models, with parameter scans focused on MeV-scale FOPTs and NANOGrav/PTArcade compatibility.
papers.similarity_to_target: Same conformal PTA target and supercooling logic, but Abelian rather than non-Abelian and not the minimal SU(2)_D doublet model.
experimental_constraints: NANOGrav 15-year dataset via PTArcade default ceffyl configuration as stated in the paper

benchmark_points.label: Table I best-fit point for the paper's U(1)' model
benchmark_points.notes: Adaptable only as PTA-scale conformal-FOPT guidance; not a direct SU(2)_D doublet benchmark.

assumptions: Daisy corrections are reported negligible in the supercooled regime discussed by this paper.
Abelian U(1)' masses and couplings are not directly transferable to SU(2)_D.

papers.title: Does NANOGrav observe a dark sector phase transition?
papers.brief_description: General analysis of whether a MeV-scale dark-sector FOPT can explain the NANOGrav signal under BBN/CMB energy-release constraints.
papers.model_studied: Phenomenological dark-sector phase transition framework rather than the target SU(2)_D scalar-doublet model.
papers.similarity_to_target: Same PTA/FOPT target and cosmological constraints; different and more general model framework.
assumptions: Used as context for viability constraints, not for target-model Lagrangian or benchmark parameters.

novelty_notes: The exact core model, a classically conformal dark SU(2)_D gauge theory with one complex scalar doublet and negligible Higgs portal, was already studied for a NANOGrav 12.5-year first-order phase-transition interpretation in arXiv:2109.11558. Novelty may remain only in using a different backend convention, updated NANOGrav 15-year/PTArcade likelihoods, omitting relic-density requirements, or scanning a different restricted parameter basis.

conventions.vev_convention: Paper uses Phi = 1/sqrt(2) (G2+iG3, M + phi + iG1)^T and states <Phi> = M/sqrt(2).
conventions.ssb_pattern: Dark scalar doublet breaks SU(2)_D and gives mass to the dark vector bosons.
conventions.field_dependent_masses: Paper reports m_D^2(phi) = g_D^2 phi^2 / 2, m_phi^2 = 3 lambda3 phi^2, m_G^2 = lambda3 phi^2.
conventions.physical_vector_mass: Paper text states M_ZD = gD M / 2.
conventions.thermal_masses: Pi_ZD = (5/6) gD^2 T^2; Pi_phi,G = (lambda3/2 + 3 gD^2/16) T^2.
conventions.effective_potential: Vtot = Vtree + VCW + VT, Landau gauge, daisy-improved finite-temperature potential.
conventions.fopt_definitions: Uses Tc, T*, Gamma(T*) = H^4(T*), S3 bounce action, alpha*, beta/H*.
conventions.gw_template: Compares envelope, semi-analytic, and numerical approximations to NANOGrav confidence regions.
conventions.pta_target_signal: NANOGrav 12.5-year FOPT interpretation, not NANOGrav 15-year/PTArcade default likelihood.

benchmark_hints.notes: Direct same-model benchmark, but downstream conversion to the proposer basis must resolve the vector-mass normalization tension.

conventions.model: Classically conformal hidden U(1)' dark sector with kinetic mixing.

benchmark_hints.notes: Useful scale guidance and constraints context only; do not map directly to SU(2)_D.

conventions.supercooling: Paper states daisy corrections are negligible in the considered supercooled regime.
conventions.pta_target_signal: NANOGrav 15-year dataset through PTArcade default ceffyl configuration.

benchmark_hints.notes: Adaptable only as a scale/template comparison for conformal PTA FOPTs; not a direct benchmark for the non-Abelian target.

constraint_summary: Same-model paper includes NANOGrav 12.5-year preferred FOPT regions and vector dark matter relic-density context.
PTA-scale dark-sector FOPT interpretations must account for BBN/CMB energy-release and Delta N_eff constraints, especially if the dark sector remains secluded or decays late.
Abelian conformal dark-sector studies show laboratory and cosmological portal constraints can dominate when a visible portal is specified.
theory_uncertainties: Gauge dependence of the perturbative finite-temperature effective potential in conformal models.
Daisy-resummation prescription dependence and supercooling treatment.
Vector-mass normalization mismatch in arXiv:2109.11558 relative to the proposer/backend convention.
NANOGrav 12.5-year versus 15-year likelihood and GW-template dependence.
Relic-density assumptions are optional for the target pipeline unless downstream scope includes dark matter abundance.
recommended_next_steps: The critic-agent should flag that the target model is not novel at the model-class level because arXiv:2109.11558 already studied it for NANOGrav.
Before FOPT implementation, reconcile the vector mass convention and use the proposer/backend convention consistently: m_WD(chi) = gD chi / 2 unless the model is intentionally changed.
Use arXiv:2109.11558 Figure 1 as the first same-model smoke benchmark, but recompute all FOPT quantities locally rather than importing paper results.
If the pipeline targets current PTA data, rerun with NANOGrav 15-year/PTArcade likelihoods rather than relying on 12.5-year contours.
Treat Abelian conformal papers only as qualitative or adaptable guidance for scale, constraints, and likelihood workflow.
